---
title: "Geomorphology"
subtitle: "Study of landforms, their processes, and the forces that shape Earth's surface."
author: "Geography Team"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---


Welcome to the **Geomorphology** module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course explores the origin, evolution, and classification of landforms across Earth's surface — from towering mountain ranges to the smallest river meanders.

---

## UGC NET Syllabus

* **Continental Drift, Plate Tectonics, Endogenetic and Exogenetic forces.**
* **Denudation and Weathering, Geomorphic Cycle (Davis and Penck).**
* **Theories and Process of Slope Development.**
* **Earth Movements (seismicity, folding, faulting and vulcanicity).**
* **Landform Occurrence and Causes of Geomorphic Hazards (earthquakes, volcanoes, landslides and avalanche).**

---

{{< include geomorphology/fundamental-concepts-of-geomorphology.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/factors-controlling-landform-development.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/endogenetic-and-exogenetic-forces.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/denudation-processes-weathering-and-erosion.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/geosynclines.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/mountain-building.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/continental-drift-and-plate-tectonics.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/concept-of-the-geomorphic-cycle.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/fluvial-cycles.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/glacial-cycles.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/arid-cycles.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/coastal-cycles.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/karst-cycles.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/slope-forms-and-processes.md >}}

{{< include geomorphology/environmental-and-applied-geomorphology.md >}}
